data.title: BlackRock spotlights investment opportunities in Japan, China, and U.K.
data.description:
BlackRock observes some compelling opportunities in global equities that are currently trading at relatively attractive valuations in areas such as Japan, China, and the United Kingdom.

BlackRock highlighted that it remains overweight Japanese stocks as the world’s largest asset manager stated: “The macro outlook [for Japan] is brightening and corporate reforms are driving improved corporate earnings and shareholder returns.”

As for the United Kingdom, the financial institution also stays overweight but does bring up some concerns. “We are overweight UK stocks, but have less conviction as the economy stagnates and heading into the new government’s budget announcement.”

China is the other avenue BlackRock is focused on, as signs of major fiscal policy stimulus are on the way. While being “modestly overweight, in China, BlackRock said, “that doesn’t change the long-term, structural challenges we are concerned about.”

For investors looking to expand from U.S. exposures, they can look towards specific nation-focused exchange traded funds. While BlackRock did not specifically mention names, Seeking Alpha constructed a list of potential investment options that would fall within exposure towards Japan, the United Kingdom, and China. See below:

Japan ETFs: (EWJ), (BBJP), (DXJ), (FLJP), (FXY), (DBJP), (HEWJ), (EWJV), (DFJ), (JPXN), (SCJ), (DXJS), (FLJH), (YCL), and (YCS).

U.K. ETFs: (EWU), (FLGB), (FKU), (FXB), and (EWUS).

China ETFs: (YANG), (KLIP), (CNYA), (ASHR), (KBA), (PGJ), (CQQQ), (CXSE), (GXC), (CHIQ), (CWEB), (YINN), (MCHI), (KWEB), and (FXI).

data.title: Sanofi unions to strike in France over proposed unit sale: report
data.description:
• Sanofi’s (NASDAQ:SNY) two largest unions in France reportedly plan to go on strike Wednesday over the proposed sale of a controlling stake in the company’s consumer healthcare unit Opella to private equity firm Clayton Dubilier & Rice for around $16B.
  • According to Reuters, the strike was called by the labor unions CGT and CFDT and will cover all of France. At issue are concerns that the deal could result in the loss of jobs and domestic production of certain medications like the pain reliever Doliprane.
  • France’s economic minister, Antoine Armand, said on Monday that his government will need certain guarantees that Doliprane production will remain in France in order for the sale to proceed, Reuters added.
  • Last week, Sanofi confirmed that it was in negotiations to sell a 50% controlling stake in Opella to Clayton Dubilier & Rice. The consumer healthcare unit, which markets such brands as Allegra and Ducolax, employs over 11,000 people and operates in 100 countries.
  • Bloomberg reported last week that Sanofi was close to selling a majority stake in Opella for around EUR 15B, or $16.4B.

data.title: Netflix subscriber addition, ad-tier growth in focus during Q3 results
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) is set to report third quarter results on Thursday, and investors will keep an eye on subscribers' trend and the performance of the streaming giant’s cheaper advertising tier.

Wall Street expects the California-based company to post EPS of $5.13, while revenue is expected to rise 14.3% to $9.76 billion during the quarter.

Netflix likely added 5.5 million net subscribers globally during the third quarter, versus consensus of 4.5 million, according to Wedbush. The streaming pioneer added 8.8 million subscribers a year earlier, which included the first full quarter impact of paid sharing.

The company plans to stop reporting subscribers number starting in the first quarter of 2025.

Despite the decelerating subscribers’ numbers, analysts are still bullish on the company’s future growth, with many anticipating planned price hikes.

Citi said it sees scope for the firm to raise U.S. prices by 12% in 2025, while Oppenheimer anticipates a hike in Premium pricing for other regions after the company’s announced price hikes in the U.S., UK, and France in October.

Earlier in July, Netflix surpassed revenue growth and user growth expectations in its second-quarter earnings.

“We think Netflix is positioned to accelerate ad tier revenue contribution into year-end and 2025 as it improves its advertising solutions and targeting, utilizes new partnerships, and adds more live events,” said Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese.

Reese also thinks Netflix will ramp up its content slate over the holiday, attracting more viewers while also managing content costs.

The company’s content slate for the quarter included a brand-new season of Emily in Paris, The Perfect Couple and The Accident, among others, while the fourth quarter will see the release of the second season of the popular South Korean drama series Squid Game.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 30 upward revisions, compared to one downward revision. Revenue estimates have been revised upward 13 times versus 19 downward moves.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street are bullish and rated the stock a Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings consider it a Hold.

The stock gained nearly 44% so far this year, outperforming the 22% rise in the S&P500 Index.

data.title: Investors should add to small- and mid-cap stocks because of valuations, Fed’s outlook – analyst
data.description:
The valuation backdrop for small-caps and mid-caps continue to look compelling for investors looking to add exposure, said Brian G. Belski, chief investment strategist at BMO Capital Markets, in a note.

Small caps (SP600) are still trading at a discount relative to their 20-year average, while mid-caps (SP400) trade only at a slight premium, according to BMO Capital Markets data. The S&P 500 (SP500), on the other hand, trades at a significant premium relative to their 20-year average.

In addition, when looking at history, small caps (SP600) have benefited once the Federal Reserve began lowering rates.

Both the S&P 600 (SP600) and S&P 400 (SP400) have averaged double-digit gains in the one-year following initial rate cuts by the Fed since 1995, at 12.8% and 10.7%, respectively, said Belski. The S&P 500 (SP500) has averaged 6.7%.

Then, average returns go over 20% for both mid caps and small caps when these rate cuts happened in economic growth, avoiding a recession.

However, currently relative performance for both cap groups is in “deeply oversold levels,” after delivering one of their best monthly performances in July. But, “from our perspective, it is only a matter of time before these trends reverse if history is any sort of guide,” he said.

This weakness has typically been followed by fast rebounds over the past 30 years, with an average outperformance of 3.1% for small-caps and 3.9% for mid-caps for all monthly rolling one-year periods.

Also, blended growth rates for small and mid-caps accelerated sharply from 2021 to 2022, with peak growth rates of 26.1% and 19.7%, respectively.

“We believe peak pessimism is in place making a rebound even more likely and recent trends have begun to show some improvement,” said Belski, and advised investors to increase exposure to the group, arguing that “it is only a matter of time before the fortunes of this group take a turn for the better.”

He said that a “highly selective approach” is necessary, and name the following outperform-rated stocks are ones fitting the theme:

  • Bunge Global SA (BG)
  • Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG)
  • Criteo SA (CRTO)
  • Dayforce, Inc. (DAY)
  • Digital Realty Trust, Inc. (DLR)
  • Entegris, Inc. (ENTG)
  • Expand Energy Corporation (EXE)
  • Exelixis, Inc. (EXEL)
  • Restaurant Brands International, Inc. (QSR)
  • Rubrik, Inc. (RBRK)
  • Ross Stores, Inc. (ROST)
  • RPM International Inc. (RPM)
  • Snap, Inc. (SNAP)
  • Trade Desk, Inc. (TTD)
  • Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (TTWO)
  • US Foods Holding Corp. (USFD)
  • Vistra Corp. (VST)

data.title: Service Properties Trust stock dives after dividend cut, plan to sell 114 hotels
data.description:
Service Properties Trust (NASDAQ:SVC) stock plunged 19% in Wednesday morning trading after the REIT slashed its dividend to $0.01 per share and plans to sell about half of its hotel properties to boost its liquidity and pay down debt.

"Given the slow recovery of our hotel portfolio in combination with our hotel capital improvement and renovation program and our deteriorating leverage metrics, we believe it is prudent to reduce the distribution to increase SVC’s liquidity and enhance our financial flexibility," said President and Chief Investment Officer Todd Hargreaves.

The company plans to sell 114 focused service hotels managed by Sonesta International Hotels in 2025 and to use the proceeds to repay debt.

SVC currently owns 187 hotels managed by Sonesta under five brands. As of June 30, 2024, Service Properties (SVC) owned a total of 220 hotels, most of which are extended stay and select service.

The dividend cut will preserve ~$127M of liquidity per year. The hotel sales are expected to save the company ~$725M in capital expenditures, which was forecast to be spent on those hotels over a six-year period.

Once the sales are completed, Sonesta is expected to continue to manage 39 SVC full-service hotels. Service Properties (SVC) will continue to own 34% of Sonesta.

The properties that are earmarked for sale have a total net carrying value of $850M and account for 14,925 keys.

In addition to the hotels, SVC owns 749 retail service-focused net lease properties in the U.S.

Service Properties (SVC) is managed by The RMR Group (RMR). RMR stock rose 2.4% in midday trading.

data.title: Brazil regulator approves sale of Tredegar's Terphane unit to Oben Group
data.description:
Tredegar (NYSE:TG) +16.3% in Wednesday's trading after saying Brazil's competition regulator approved a merger control agreement allowing the company to proceed with the sale of Terphane, its flexible packaging business headquartered in Brazil, to Oben Group.

Tredegar (TG) said the conditions contained in the merger control agreement, comprised mostly of post-closing commitments for Oben to address certain competition concerns, are expected to be met with deal closing planned for November 1.

On September 1, Tredegar (TG) announced an agreement to sell Terphane to Oben for net cash-free and debt-free base consideration of $116M.

data.title: Adicet Bio gets FDA clearance of IND amendment for stiff person syndrome treatment
data.description:
• Adicet Bio (NASDAQ:ACET) announced that the U.S. FDA has agreed to an amendment to the company’s Investigational New Drug application to evaluate ADI-001 in idiopathic inflammatory myopathy and stiff person syndrome as part of the ongoing Phase 1 trial in autoimmune diseases.
  • The company plans to initiate enrollment for IIM and SPS patients in the first quarter of 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Goldman Sachs increases China's GDP forecast to 4.9% for 2024
data.description:
Goldman Sachs updated its forecast for China's GDP growth in 2024, raising it from 4.7% to 4.9%. This adjustment reflects the impact of recent fiscal stimulus measures introduced by the Chinese government aimed at revitalizing the economy.

In addition to the 2024 forecast, Goldman Sachs also revised its 2025 GDP growth estimate to 4.7%, up from a previous prediction of 4.3%.

Key elements that supported the bank's outlook revision on China include: "First, the Ministry of Finance press conference on October 12 suggested that RMB2.3tn of local government special bond funds will be used in Q4, suggesting a more back-loaded fiscal impulse and a larger rebound in growth from Q3 to Q4 than we previously anticipated. Second, the National Development and Reform Commission stated it would pre-approve RMB200bn of next year’s projects by end-October, presumably to begin construction before year-end, an effort to get real GDP growth closer to this year’s “around 5%” target in our view," the analyst said.

The investment bank's analysts noted that these measures indicate a shift in policy management, with a renewed focus on economic growth.

In a much-awaited briefing on Saturday, the Finance Ministry pledged more fiscal support, but it did not include measures to increase consumption.

"While we have upgraded our cyclical view on the back of the more forceful and coordinated China stimulus, our structural view on China’s growth has not changed. The “3D” challenges - deteriorating demographics, a multi-year debt deleveraging trend, and the global supply chain de-risking push are unlikely to be reversed by the latest round of policy easing. Our growth forecasts for 2026 and beyond remain unchanged," the Goldman Sachs report outlined.

The investment bank has maintained Q3 real GDP growth at 4.5%. The world's second-largest economy is set to report Q3 GDP numbers on Friday, October 18.

The Chinese economy expanded 4.7% Y/Y in Q2 of 2024, missing market forecasts of 5.1% and slowing from a 5.3% growth in Q1, the weakest yearly advance since Q1 of 2023, amid a persistent property downturn, weak domestic demand, falling yuan, and trade frictions with the West.

China-based tickers to follow: ETFs: (FXI), (KWEB), (CQQQ), (MCHI), (ASHR), (YINN), (TDF), (CHIQ), (GXC), (EWH), (KBA), (YANG), (CXSE), (CAF), (CWEB), (PGJ), (KURE).

data.title: Italy said to lift taxes on capital gains from bitcoin to 42% from 26%
data.description:
Deputy Finance Minister Maurizio Leo said Wednesday that Italy plans to hike capital gains tax on cryptocurrencies like bitcoin (BTC-USD) to 42% from 26%, according to several media reports.

The price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) appeared unfazed by the news, having risen 2.4% to $67.6K in late morning trading. The token is up some 53% so far this year.

Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s cabinet took this action as the “phenomenon is spreading,” Leo said of bitcoin (BTC-USD) during a conference call, Bloomberg reported.

The decision aligns with Italy's plans to bolster its digital services tax to boost revenue for the government's 2025 budget.

But previous attempts by other countries to tax crypto trading have not always been a boon for government budgets.

In 2022, for example, India imposed a tax on income from crypto assets and non-fungible tokens. That prompted investors to transition to offshore platforms to circumvent the tax, a move that pulled down trading volumes in India, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Vestas rises on pricing pact by Chinese rivals; wind turbine catches fire in Denmark
data.description:
Vestas Wind (OTCPK:VWDRY) +2.6% in Wednesday's trading following news that several of China's biggest wind turbine manufacturers signed an agreement seeking to stop the price war in the wind turbine market.

Fear of competition from China has contributed to pressure on Vestas' (OTCPK:VWDRY) share price, which has fallen 40% YTD.

Separately, Vestas (OTCPK:VWDRY) said a wind turbine at Bjerringbro in western Denmark that was produced by the company had caught fire, but no one was injured and the rest of its fleet was working as normal.

The company said the cause of the fire was not yet known; local authorities said it was not possible to extinguish the flames and that the turbine would be allowed to burn out on its own.
